page: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-32 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs, operating at a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio, which ensures no resource sharing between threads on the same core. The instance is configured with 128 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs.

Benchmark evaluations place this instance in the top 25% for multi-core and single-core CPU performance via stress-ng, as well as PassMark CPU metrics, including encryption and physics tests. Small-block cached memory reads are highly efficient, reaching 3955.45 GB/sec, though large-block uncached memory read and write speeds fall into the average tier. Cryptographic operations benefit from hardware-accelerated instructions like AES and SHA_NI.

This server is well-suited for database operations, image processing, and small-scale language model inference, where it demonstrates strong prompt processing and text generation speeds. However, the lack of local NVMe storage and GPUs limits its applicability for heavy storage-bound databases or large-scale deep learning training. It represents a cost-efficient option for general-purpose workloads requiring dedicated, non-hyperthreaded CPU execution.
description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-32 is a general-purpose server instance configured with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM, utilizing the x86_64 AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. Operating with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio, it delivers dedicated physical core execution without hyper-threading. The hardware profile lacks local storage and GPUs, focusing instead on balanced compute and memory resources. In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates strong multi-core CPU performance, high database operation throughput, and efficient small-block cached memory bandwidth, placing it in the top tier for compute-heavy tasks. Conversely, large-block uncached memory operations and software compilation fall into average performance bands. This instance is highly suited for database hosting, image processing, and moderate machine learning inference tasks that require consistent, dedicated CPU performance without the overhead of shared-thread architectures.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-32 is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC 7B13 vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM, delivering strong multi-core performance for database and image processing workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-32 features 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 128 GB RAM, optimized for database and general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A dedicated 32-core AMD EPYC processor instance offering balanced memory and strong multi-core performance for general-purpose and database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 32-core AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with dedicated physical cores.
Equipped with 128 GB of system memory at a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio.
Strong multi-core CPU performance and database operation throughput.
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the default configuration.
Best suited for database hosting, image processing, and moderate LLM inference.
